task_completion.description: Open and read the spacecraft power specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of 'skywatch3_power_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Configure the spacecraft orbit parameters in GMAT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing the Spacecraft resource with Keplerian elements matching the specification (SMA ~6878.14, INC ~97.42, RAAN ~195.0) or a script editor showing these values.

task_completion.description: Set up the Eclipse Locator and 30-day propagation in GMAT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing an EclipseLocator resource configured, or a Mission Sequence showing a Propagate command set for 30 days.

task_completion.description: Save the GMAT simulation script to the required directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or GMAT save dialog showing 'skywatch3_eclipse.script' located in the '~/GMAT_output/' directory.

task_completion.description: Run the GMAT simulation to generate eclipse data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT message window showing a successful run, an active orbit animation window, or an opened EclipseLocator output text file showing umbra/penumbra events.

task_completion.description: Write the final eclipse analysis report with all required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'eclipse_analysis_report.txt' containing the fields: num_eclipses, max_eclipse_min, avg_eclipse_min, eclipse_fraction, required_Wh, margin_percent, and battery_adequate, with values populated.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used GMAT to perform the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GMAT application open and being interacted with, rather than the agent just writing a Python script or guessing the values.

integrity.description: Verify the report contains calculated values, not placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'eclipse_analysis_report.txt' should have specific numerical values for the eclipse statistics that align with the GMAT output, not generic text like '[insert max eclipse here]'.

integrity.description: Verify files are saved in the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths for the script and report must explicitly show 'GMAT_output' as the parent folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spacecraft orbit parameters are SMA: 6878.14 km, INC: 97.42 deg, RAAN: 195.0 deg. The expected eclipse statistics for the 30-day simulation are approximately: 455 eclipses (allowed range: 400-520), max eclipse duration: 35.3 minutes (allowed range: 30.0-42.0), average eclipse duration: 35.25 minutes (allowed range: 28.0-40.0), and eclipse fraction: 0.371 (allowed range: 0.3-0.44). The final report must be at ~/GMAT_output/eclipse_analysis_report.txt and contain the fields: num_eclipses, max_eclipse_min, avg_eclipse_min, eclipse_fraction, required_Wh, margin_percent, and battery_adequate.
